title: MedPanel Shared Definitions
description: Single source of truth for all enums and reusable types. Every other schema references these via $ref. Resolves vocabulary fragmentation across specs, prompts, and schemas.

evidence_tier.description:
Unified evidence quality scale. Maps GRADE (high/moderate/low/very_low), supplement tiers (S/A/B/C/D), and clinical tiers (strong/moderate/preliminary/mechanistic/unknown) into one system. Agents output this single scale; the moderator does not need cross-vocabulary mapping.

  Mapping from legacy systems:
    GRADE high           → strong
    GRADE moderate        → moderate
    Supplement Tier S     → strong
    Supplement Tier A     → moderate
    GRADE low / Tier B    → preliminary
    GRADE very_low / Tier C → mechanistic_or_theoretical
    Tier D / unknown      → insufficient
    expert_opinion        → expert_opinion
    extrapolated          → extrapolated

  Functional medicine's custom tiers:
    tier_1_conventional_agreement → strong
    tier_2_plausible              → moderate
    tier_3_theoretical            → mechanistic_or_theoretical
    tier_4_unsupported            → insufficient

severity.description:
Unified severity scale for safety flags, risk flags, interactions, and any escalation trigger. Resolves 5 conflicting scales:
  - agent-output.json safety_flags: critical/warning/caution/informational
  - DISCUSSION-PROTOCOL risk_flags: red/orange/yellow/green
  - consultation.json drug interactions: contraindicated/major/moderate/minor
  - patient-profile conditions: mild/moderate/severe
  - cross_examination disagreements: fundamental/nuanced/minor

  Mapping:
    critical / red / contraindicated → critical
    warning / orange / major         → high
    caution / yellow / moderate       → moderate
    informational / green / minor     → low
    (no flag)                         → informational

confidence.description: Numeric confidence score 0.0-1.0. Chosen over string enum because: (1) the DISCUSSION-PROTOCOL spec uses numeric confidence (0.92, 0.78) for reproducibility comparison, threshold checks, and false consensus detection; (2) numeric allows computing averages, deltas, and budget-linked degradation decisions; (3) the spec's are_outputs_acceptably_similar() function compares numeric confidence deltas. The old string enum (high/moderate/low/uncertain) maps approximately to: high ≥ 0.80, moderate 0.50-0.79, low 0.25-0.49, uncertain < 0.25.

specialist_type.description: Complete list of all specialist types. Includes the original 6 plus internist (always-included anchor), and all specialists from the DISCUSSION-PROTOCOL DOMAIN_MAP and QUESTION-CLASSIFICATION DOMAIN_TO_SPECIALIST that were missing from the old schema (sports_medicine, sleep_medicine, hepatologist, immunologist, nutritional_medicine, gastroenterologist, hematologist, rheumatologist, dermatologist, reproductive_endocrinologist, ophthalmologist, otolaryngologist, pulmonologist, pain_management, geriatrician, obstetrician).

consultation_type.description: Unified consultation type. Resolves the mismatch between consultation.json classification.intent and DISCUSSION-PROTOCOL consultation_type. Uses the classifier's 8-value enum (Axis 1) as canonical because it is the entry point for every consultation and has the most precise definitions and few-shot examples.

urgency.description: Urgency level from classifier (Axis 3) and safety system. The safety system can escalate urgency but classification can never downgrade below the safety system's determination.

domain.description: Organ system / body domain codes from the classification spec (Axis 2). Maps to ICD-10 chapter structures. Used in classifier output and evidence routing.

verification_status.description: PubMed PMID verification result. From EVIDENCE-PIPELINE.md Step 3 verification flow.

study_type.description: Type of clinical study. Used in citations and evidence package.

medication_type.description: Classification of substances in the patient's medication/supplement list.

interaction_source.description: Which database identified a drug/supplement interaction.

source_confidence.description: Reliability of an interaction data source.

retrieval_step_status.description: Status of an individual evidence pipeline step.

retrieval_overall_status.description: Overall health of the evidence retrieval pipeline.

consultation_status.description: Lifecycle status of a consultation session.

resolution_type.description: How a Round 3 disagreement was resolved.

consensus_level.description: Degree of agreement in the moderator's synthesis.

agreement_level.description: Level of specialist agreement on a consensus item.

model_tier.description: LLM model tier assignment for a specialist.

citation.description: A single evidence citation. Agents reference these by citation_id from the evidence package; they do not fabricate new citations from memory.

citation_id.description: Unique identifier within the evidence package (e.g., 'C-001'). Agents reference this ID instead of fabricating citations.

pmid.description: PubMed ID. Null if not available or fabricated.

claim.description: The specific claim this citation supports

pubmed_metadata.description: Actual PubMed metadata (if verified). Agents prefer this over search-engine-claimed metadata.

abstract_excerpt.description: First 500 chars of abstract for claim verification

interaction.description: A drug-drug, drug-supplement, or supplement-supplement interaction.

references.description: PMIDs or URLs supporting this interaction

lab_result.description: A single lab result with reference ranges and interpretation.

trend.description: Requires 2+ readings. From DISCUSSION-PROTOCOL evidence package spec.

safety_flag.description: A safety concern identified by a specialist or the safety system.

budget_tier.description: Tiered consultation budget ceiling. Replaces the flat $5 ceiling which is mathematically impossible for complex Opus-heavy cases. Based on complexity score ranges from QUESTION-CLASSIFICATION.md.
